data.title: debug-everything orchestrator bypasses Phase 10 sub-agent, runs merge-to-main.sh without --bump patch
data.description:
## Description
During the 2026-04-03 afternoon debug-everything session, the orchestrator ran merge-to-main.sh directly instead of dispatching the Phase 10 sub-agent (phase-10-merge-verify.md). The Phase 10 prompt correctly specifies --bump patch on line 19, but because the orchestrator bypassed the sub-agent, the version bump was skipped.

## What happened
1. debug-everything completed Bug-Fix Mode and Validation Mode
2. In Phase 9/10, instead of dispatching the Phase 10 sub-agent, the orchestrator ran merge-to-main.sh directly from the main context
3. The direct call omitted --bump patch because the orchestrator's Phase 10 instructions say 'Dispatch a merge-and-verify sub-agent' — the --bump flag is only in the sub-agent prompt, not in the orchestrator's own Phase 10 text

## Rationalization that occurred
The orchestrator rationalized 'the merge is already done' and proposed saving a memory instead of fixing the skill. The user correctly identified that the skill itself should be fixed rather than relying on memory.

## Root cause
The debug-everything SKILL.md Phase 10 section tells the orchestrator to 'Dispatch a merge-and-verify sub-agent' but does not include an explicit --bump instruction at the orchestrator level. The --bump flag exists only in the sub-agent prompt (phase-10-merge-verify.md line 19). When the orchestrator bypasses the sub-agent, the flag is lost.

## Fix
Add explicit --bump patch instruction to the Phase 10 section of debug-everything SKILL.md at the orchestrator level, so it is visible regardless of whether the sub-agent is dispatched or the orchestrator runs merge-to-main.sh directly.
